mnemonics: N.O.R.M.A.L. stands for Naturally Occurring Real-world Modeled Around Limits
Remember the 'bell' shape by thinking of a bell that rings 'mean, median, mode' all at once.
keyPoints: The normal distribution is symmetric and defined by its mean (μ) and standard deviation (σ).
Approximately 68%, 95%, and 99.7% of data falls within 1, 2, and 3 standard deviations of the mean, respectively (Empirical Rule).
Many statistical tests and machine learning models assume data is normally distributed or can be transformed to be so.
commonMistakes: Assuming a dataset is normal without performing a test for normality (e.g., Q-Q plot, Shapiro-Wilk).
Applying techniques that assume normality to data with extreme outliers or a heavily skewed distribution.
